summary: Tested Firebase Google Auth login page UI and flow. Login page renders correctly but Google Sign-In fails with auth/unauthorized-domain error. Theme toggle is absent from login page (only available post-login in Header). All other login page UI elements are present and well-designed.

ui_bugs.issue: Theme toggle is NOT present on the login page. Feature request in review says 'Theme toggle still works on both login page and main app' but there is no theme toggle on LoginPage.jsx - it only exists in Header.jsx (post-login).

Loading spinner does not visually appear after clicking 'Continue with Google'. The Firebase auth/unauthorized-domain error fires immediately, resetting loading state before spinner can be seen. The spinner code exists (animate-spin div) but fires and resets too quickly.

integration_issues.issue: CRITICAL: Firebase Error (auth/unauthorized-domain) when clicking Continue with Google on mobile (and desktop popup). The domain 'auto-task-master.preview.emergentagent.com' is NOT added to Firebase Console > Authentication > Settings > Authorized domains. This completely blocks login.

issues: Clean design with left features panel, right card with Google sign-in. Good contrast and spacing. No major design issues.
Mobile layout renders correctly with TaskAI logo and Continue with Google button. Error toast (Firebase unauthorized domain) overlays top of screen.

action_items: CRITICAL: Add 'auto-task-master.preview.emergentagent.com' to Firebase Console > Authentication > Settings > Authorized domains. Without this, Google Sign-In completely fails.
MEDIUM: Add theme toggle button to LoginPage.jsx if the intent is to have theme switching on the login page too.
LOW: After domain fix, verify the loading spinner is visible for a brief moment during sign-in popup
critical_code_review_comments: Firebase auth domain not authorized - this is a configuration issue in Firebase Console, not a code issue. The code itself is correct.

LoginPage has no theme toggle - AppContent renders LoginPage directly without passing theme/onToggleTheme props. If dark mode is needed on login page, theme toggle needs to be added to LoginPage component.
App.js L56: if (!user) return <LoginPage /> - no theme prop passed to LoginPage, so theme state is inaccessible on the login screen.
AuthContext.js wraps app with onAuthStateChanged - good pattern. Auth loading spinner implemented correctly.
Firestore real-time listener with onSnapshot is correct and properly unsubscribed on cleanup.

success_rate.frontend: 50% - Login page UI renders correctly but Google auth fails due to unauthorized domain

context_for_next_testing_agent: The critical blocker is Firebase unauthorized domain error. Once the domain 'auto-task-master.preview.emergentagent.com' is added to Firebase Console authorized domains, re-test: (1) Google Sign-In popup flow, (2) Post-login main app with user avatar in header, (3) User dropdown menu with logout, (4) Firestore skeleton loading (3 placeholder cards), (5) API key dialog, (6) Theme toggle in main app. Previous iterations (1 & 2) tested non-Firebase features of the app.
rca_of_the_issue: Root Cause: Firebase Authentication requires domains to be explicitly whitelisted in Firebase Console > Authentication > Settings > Authorized domains. The preview environment domain 'auto-task-master.preview.emergentagent.com' was never added. Reproduction: Click 'Continue with Google' on login page -> Firebase immediately throws auth/unauthorized-domain error. Fix: Add the domain in Firebase Console (requires Firebase project owner access - this is a console configuration, not a code change).
